title: 10 Heavily Undervalued Stocks Trading Just Above Their 200-Day Moving Average
author: David Wagner
description: The article identifies 10 large-cap US stocks trading just above their 200-day moving average while showing undervaluation of 26.4% to 39.9%. It highlights three specific stocks: Jones Lang LaSalle (JLL) with strong momentum in commercial real estate and a new growth plan, Assurant (AIZ) showing solid financial performance with analyst 'Strong Buy' ratings, and Becton Dickinson (BDX) benefiting from its shift toward higher-value medical technologies. The article explains how the 200-day moving average can serve as a technical analysis tool for identifying entry and exit points.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong momentum in rental markets and capital transactions recovery, new 'Accelerate 2030' plan targeting 16% annual adjusted EPS growth, expanded $2.2 billion share buyback program, trading at 24% discount to analyst price targets and 36% to intrinsic value

Solid financial performance with Connected Living division reporting ~14% revenue growth and ~21% EBITDA growth, analyst 'Strong Buy' view with average price target suggesting over 9% upside, low volatility with beta of 0.54

Strong positioning following shift toward higher-value medical technologies under 'New BD' strategy, Q1 2026 results exceeded expectations, stock remains undervalued despite recent pullback from highs

title: JLL Adopts ARGUS Intelligence as Continuation of Existing Partnership
author: Altus Group Limited
description: Jones Lang LaSalle (JLL) has renewed its relationship with Altus Group to use ARGUS Intelligence for valuation requirements across its Capital Markets & Investment Services businesses globally. The expanded agreement includes an asset-based licensing model and exploration of advanced performance analysis tools, alongside Altus' Forbury product already in use in Australia.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The renewal of an existing partnership is a routine business decision that maintains operational continuity. While it shows confidence in Altus' products, it represents a continuation rather than a significant new development for JLL.

title: Commercial Real Estate Recovery: Goldman Sachs Names 3 Top Stocks For 2025
author: Piero Cingari
description: Goldman Sachs is bullish on the commercial real estate services sector, citing a gradual recovery in property values, financing conditions, and origination volumes. The investment bank recommends CBRE Group, Newmark Group, and Jones Lang LaSalle as top picks for 2025.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Goldman Sachs believes CBRE's diversified business model and resilient growth across property management, facilities management, and project management make it a standout in the sector.
Goldman Sachs highlights Newmark's ascent in mortgage origination rankings and its growing capital markets business, which has led to market share gains.
Goldman Sachs believes JLL is benefiting from its increased market share in investment sales, which puts the company in a prime position for earnings growth.

title: Can CBRE Group (CBRE) Keep Its Winning Streak Alive in Q2? - Zacks Investment Research
author: Zacks Investment Research
description: CBRE Group, Inc. is set to announce its second-quarter 2024 earnings on July 25. The company is expected to benefit from its diversified business model, technology investments, and cost reduction initiatives, though persistent macroeconomic uncertainties may impact commercial real estate transactions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article suggests that CBRE Group is expected to benefit from its diversified business model, technology investments, and cost reduction initiatives, indicating a positive outlook for the company.
The article mentions Jones Lang LaSalle Incorporated as another company in the real estate operations industry, but does not provide any specific details about its performance or outlook.
The article mentions Cushman & Wakefield plc as another company in the real estate operations industry, but does not provide any specific details about its performance or outlook.

title: IBM, Jones Lang Unite to Boost ESG Reporting in the CRE Sector - Zacks Investment Research
author: Zacks Equity Research
description: IBM and Jones Lang LaSalle have launched a new sustainability solution to address the growing demand for transparent and accurate ESG data in the commercial real estate sector. The collaboration aims to help organizations capture and manage data across real estate portfolios for better decision-making and reporting.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights IBM's collaboration with Jones Lang LaSalle to launch a new sustainability solution, which is expected to improve the profitability of IBM and strengthen its leadership in delivering innovative solutions across the hybrid cloud, AI and consulting services.

The article states that the collaboration with IBM will likely help Jones Lang LaSalle's sustainability experts deliver robust reporting and enhanced data management to their clients for decarbonization strategies, which is seen as a positive development.
The article mentions Telephone and Data Systems, Inc. as a better-ranked stock in the industry, which suggests a positive sentiment towards the company.
